title: Accountants and auditors
exposure_rationale: The core work of accountants is highly digital and involves significant routine information processing, making it a prime candidate for AI-driven automation. However, the role remains protected by high requirements for professional judgment, legal accountability, and the need for human-centric advisory and communication skills when interacting with clients and stakeholders.

title: Actors
exposure_rationale: Acting is fundamentally rooted in physical presence, live performance, and complex human expression, which are difficult to replicate entirely through digital means. While digital tools are used in production and voiceover work, the core of the profession relies on human creativity, interpersonal collaboration, and the physical embodiment of characters.

title: Actuaries
exposure_rationale: Actuarial work is highly digital and involves significant routine information processing through statistical modeling and database analysis. However, the role requires high-stakes professional judgment, regulatory accountability, and the ability to communicate complex findings to stakeholders, which limits the extent to which the entire job can be automated.

title: Administrative services and facilities managers
exposure_rationale: This role balances digital administrative tasks and data management with significant physical oversight of facilities and infrastructure. Success relies heavily on interpersonal leadership, coordination of diverse staff, and high-stakes judgment regarding safety, compliance, and organizational efficiency.

title: Adult basic and secondary education and ESL teachers
exposure_rationale: While these teachers use digital tools for lesson planning and documentation, the core of the role relies heavily on in-person instruction, empathy, and the ability to adapt to diverse student needs in real-time. The high dependency on human interaction and the professional judgment required to assess and support adult learners make this role less susceptible to full automation.

title: Advertising sales agents
exposure_rationale: The role is highly digital in its delivery and management of advertising assets, but the core value proposition relies heavily on human persuasion, rapport-building, and negotiation. While routine tasks like drafting contracts and processing paperwork are susceptible to automation, the high-stakes interpersonal nature of sales and the need for customized client solutions remain central to the occupation.

title: Advertising, promotions, and marketing managers
exposure_rationale: The role is highly digital and involves significant information processing, such as analyzing market data and managing digital campaigns, which are susceptible to automation. However, the core of the job relies heavily on high-stakes human interaction, strategic persuasion, and professional judgment in managing client relationships and creative vision, which are difficult to fully delegate to AI.

title: Aerospace engineering and operations technologists and technicians
exposure_rationale: While this role involves significant digital interaction through computer simulations and data recording, the core of the work is heavily dependent on physical manipulation, assembly, and maintenance of aerospace hardware in real-world environments. The high stakes associated with aerospace safety and the need for precise, hands-on troubleshooting create a strong requirement for human judgment and accountability that is difficult to fully automate.

title: Aerospace engineers
exposure_rationale: Aerospace engineering relies heavily on digital design, simulation, and modeling tools, though the work is fundamentally tethered to physical safety, testing, and real-world performance. The role requires high-stakes professional judgment and accountability for complex systems where errors can have catastrophic consequences, making it difficult to fully automate despite the high degree of digital information processing.

title: Agricultural and food science technicians
exposure_rationale: The role is heavily dependent on physical interaction with samples, laboratory equipment, and agricultural environments, which limits the scope for AI automation. While data recording and analysis are routine and digital, the core of the job requires manual manipulation and on-site observation that cannot be fully replaced by digital systems.

title: Agricultural and food scientists
exposure_rationale: While these scientists use digital tools for data analysis and reporting, their work is fundamentally anchored in physical-world experimentation, field sampling, and site-specific inspections. The role requires high-stakes professional judgment regarding food safety, environmental impact, and biological research that cannot be fully automated or delegated to AI.

title: Agricultural engineers
exposure_rationale: Agricultural engineers rely heavily on digital design and simulation software, but their work is fundamentally anchored in physical-world applications like machinery, livestock facilities, and environmental management. The role requires high-stakes professional judgment and accountability, particularly for licensed engineers, while balancing technical analysis with the need to coordinate across diverse stakeholder groups.

title: Agricultural workers
exposure_rationale: The core of this occupation is manual labor, physical interaction with crops and livestock, and the operation of machinery in outdoor environments. While some record-keeping exists, the work is overwhelmingly defined by physical presence and manual dexterity rather than digital or information-based tasks.

exposure_rationale: The role is highly digital and involves constant processing of structured information (radar, flight plans, weather data) to manage traffic flow. However, it remains deeply dependent on physical-world outcomes and high-stakes, real-time human judgment, where the accountability for safety makes full automation extremely difficult to implement.

title: Aircraft and avionics equipment mechanics and technicians
exposure_rationale: The core of this occupation is highly physical, requiring manual manipulation of complex machinery in real-world environments. While they use digital tools for diagnostics and record-keeping, the high-stakes nature of aviation safety necessitates human professional judgment and accountability that cannot be easily delegated to AI.

title: Airline and commercial pilots
exposure_rationale: While pilots rely heavily on digital cockpit instrumentation and navigation systems, the core of the job is physically operating an aircraft in a high-stakes, real-world environment. The role requires immense professional accountability and split-second human judgment that is difficult to delegate, even as automation handles routine flight path management.

title: Animal care and service workers
exposure_rationale: The core of this occupation is physical interaction with animals, requiring manual labor, direct observation, and site-specific care that cannot be digitized or automated. While there is some administrative record-keeping and client communication, the primary value is derived from the physical presence and specialized handling of living creatures.

title: Announcers and DJs
exposure_rationale: Broadcast announcers and DJs rely heavily on digital production software and media delivery, making them highly susceptible to automation in content generation and scheduling. exposure_rationale: While these professionals use digital tools for analysis and reporting, their core work is heavily anchored in physical fieldwork, excavation, and direct observation of human behavior or artifacts. The role requires significant high-stakes judgment in interpreting complex cultural or historical data and navigating interpersonal relationships during research, which limits the potential for full automation.

title: Arbitrators, mediators, and conciliators
exposure_rationale: The core of this role relies on high-stakes human interaction, empathy, and neutral judgment, which are difficult to automate. exposure_rationale: While these managers rely heavily on digital tools for planning and design, their core value lies in high-stakes leadership, team coordination, and complex decision-making that requires deep domain expertise. Their work is deeply tied to physical-world outcomes like construction and manufacturing, necessitating a blend of technical oversight and interpersonal management that is difficult to fully automate.

title: Assemblers and fabricators
exposure_rationale: The core of this occupation is manual labor and physical manipulation of components in a manufacturing environment, making it highly dependent on the physical world. While workers interact with digital schematics and automated systems, the primary value is derived from physical assembly, quality control, and dexterity rather than information processing or interpersonal relationships.

title: Athletes and sports competitors
exposure_rationale: The core of this occupation is physical performance, training, and competition, which are inherently tied to the physical world and cannot be digitized or automated. title: Automotive service technicians and mechanics
exposure_rationale: While automotive technicians increasingly use computerized diagnostic tools, the core of the job remains highly physical, requiring manual manipulation of heavy machinery and complex mechanical systems. exposure_rationale: Baking is a highly manual, physical occupation centered on the transformation of raw ingredients into food products in a real-world environment. While some bakers manage business operations or interact with customers, the core of the role involves physical labor, sensory evaluation of products, and manual manipulation of materials that are largely resistant to digital or AI-driven automation.

title: Barbers, hairstylists, and cosmetologists

title: Bartenders
exposure_rationale: Bartending is highly dependent on physical presence, manual dexterity, and real-world interaction, making it resistant to automation in its core service delivery. title: Biochemists and biophysicists
exposure_rationale: While this role involves significant digital data analysis and modeling, the core of the work remains rooted in physical laboratory experimentation and the manipulation of biological materials. exposure_rationale: The core of this role is highly dependent on physical laboratory work, manual manipulation of equipment, and biological sample handling, which limits AI's ability to replace the physical tasks. title: Chefs and head cooks
exposure_rationale: The core of this occupation is highly dependent on physical presence, manual dexterity, and sensory evaluation of food in a real-world kitchen environment. title: Chemical engineers

title: Chemical technicians
exposure_rationale: The role is heavily anchored in physical laboratory work and equipment maintenance, which limits the scope for full automation. title: Chemists and materials scientists
exposure_rationale: While these professionals increasingly use digital modeling and simulation tools, their core work remains deeply rooted in physical laboratory experimentation, material manipulation, and real-world safety testing. title: Childcare workers
exposure_rationale: The core of this occupation is physical, hands-on care for children, which requires constant physical presence and high-stakes, real-time decision-making regarding safety and development. title: Chiropractors
exposure_rationale: The core of this occupation is physical, hands-on manipulation of the human body, which cannot be automated or digitized. title: Civil engineers
